introSection: Skyclone was a wooden roller coaster located at Central Park in Allentown, Pennsylvania, USA. It was built by H.A. Basher, a company based in Lancaster, Pennsylvania.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The attraction opened as Cyclone on May 14, 1927.

Much of the attraction was damaged in a fire in September 1935. It was rebuilt and opened on May 11, 1936 with a new name, Skyclone. The name "Skyclone" was suggested by a member of the public as part of a competition. Skyclone was destroyed by another fire on May 18, 1944, and was not rebuilt.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The first drop was a double down, said to be tall, which took advantage of a natural valley.

fullText:
Skyclone was a wooden roller coaster located at Central Park in Allentown, Pennsylvania, USA. It was built by H.A. Basher, a company based in Lancaster, Pennsylvania.

The attraction opened as Cyclone on May 14, 1927.

Much of the attraction was damaged in a fire in September 1935. It was rebuilt and opened on May 11, 1936 with a new name, Skyclone. The name "Skyclone" was suggested by a member of the public as part of a competition. Skyclone was destroyed by another fire on May 18, 1944, and was not rebuilt.

The first drop was a double down, said to be tall, which took advantage of a natural valley.
